decks.notes: Fast-paced Boros aggro deck focused on quickly building a board presence and overwhelming the opponent with efficient creatures and burn spells. Utilizing powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Guide of Souls, along with aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Ocelot Pride, the deck aims to close out the game quickly. Support cards like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment provide additional reach and removal, while disruptive spells like Mana Tithe and Thraben Charm help control the board. The inclusion of legendary creatures and enchantments adds versatility and resilience to the strategy.

The deck focuses on utilizing a toolbox of creatures to set up various combo interactions and control the board. It aims to quickly assemble powerful combinations of creatures with abilities that synergize well with each other, creating a versatile and adaptable strategy. The deck includes a mix of creatures with different abilities and utility spells to help find and protect key pieces. By assembling the right combination of creatures and utilizing their abilities effectively, the deck can outvalue and outmaneuver opponents to secure victory.

The deck revolves around quickly assembling a board presence of artifacts, fueled by cards like Mox Opal and Mishra's Bauble. Using cards like Kappa Cannoneer and Pinnacle Emissary to pressure the opponent quickly, while also having various utility artifacts to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck also leans heavily on Urza's Saga to create additional artifact threats. With a mix of aggro and disruption elements, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can establish their own game plan.

The deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with low-cost artifacts and aggressive creatures, utilizing cards like Kappa Cannoneer and Pinnacle Emissary to put pressure early in the game. Emry, Lurker of the Loch helps recur artifacts from the graveyard, while cards like Engineered Explosives and Tormod's Crypt provide disruption against the opponent's strategy. The deck also includes tools for protection and disruption, such as Claws of Gix and Metallic Rebuke. Urza's Saga serves as a powerful engine to search for key artifacts and generate advantage. Overall, the deck aims to win through a combination of speed, synergy, and disruption.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly outpace the opponent through aggressive creature plays and utilizing blink effects to generate additional value from entering the battlefield triggers. By combining cards that can blink creatures like Ephemerate and Teferi, Time Raveler with impactful creatures like Starfield Shepherd and Witch Enchanter, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a steady stream of threats. Additionally, the deck includes powerful removal spells like Solitude and Consign to Memory to maintain board control while pressuring opponents.

This deck focuses on disruption, removal, and controlling the game through counterspells and hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize. It also utilizes creatures with powerful abilities like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Tasigur, the Golden Fang to maintain control. The inclusion of cards like Drown in the Loch and Fatal Push help to deal with threats on the board, while cards like Subtlety and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provide additional versatility. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue and outlast opponents through a combination of disruption and creature threats.

The deck focuses on quickly getting powerful creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Griselbrand onto the battlefield through instant reanimation spells like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate. It also includes disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation to protect the combo. The deck has a strong control element with cards like Psychic Frog and Quantum Riddler to slow down the opponent's game plan. The deck also includes some removal options like Solitude and Prismatic Ending to deal with threats on the board. Overall, the deck aims to combo off quickly and disrupt the opponent's strategy.

The deck focuses on utilizing ramp and cascade effects to quickly play powerful, disruptive creatures and maintain control over the board. By casting spells for free or at a reduced cost, the deck can overwhelm opponents and disrupt their game plan while steadily building up their own board presence. The combination of creatures like Abhorrent Oculus and Coiling Oracle with counterspells like Flare of Denial and Subtlety ensures that the deck can both apply pressure and protect itself from removal or opposing threats.

The deck focuses on quickly deploying artifacts onto the battlefield to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of creature aggression and disruptive spells. Cards like Kappa Cannoneer, Pinnacle Emissary, and Arcbound Ravager provide a strong attacking force, while Engineered Explosives, Metallic Rebuke, and Tormod's Crypt disrupt the opponent's strategy. The goal is to quickly assemble a board presence and disrupt the opponent's game plan through a combination of aggressive creatures and disruption spells, ultimately overwhelming the opponent before they can stabilize.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying efficient threats while maintaining board advantage with removal spells. The key strategy is to pressure the opponent early with creatures like Ragavan and Ocelot Pride, while using Ajani, Guide of Souls, and Ranger-Captain of Eos to provide value and maintain card advantage. Phelia and Boromir provide additional power and protection, while Fable of the Mirror-Breaker and Goblin Bombardment offer flexibility in dealing with opposing threats. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive game plan.

This deck focuses on quickly building a board presence and overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. It utilizes creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Ocelot Pride to apply pressure early on, while also using burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Sundering Eruption to clear the way for attacks. The inclusion of Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ranger-Captain of Eos provide additional utility and card advantage. Overall, the deck aims to maintain a fast pace and close out games before the opponent has a chance to stabilize.

The deck focuses on aggressive creatures and spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It includes powerful creatures like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer, along with support cards like Ranger-Captain of Eos and Seasoned Pyromancer. With a mix of burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment, along with disruption from cards like Thraben Charm and Static Prison, the deck aims to apply early pressure and finish off the opponent quickly. The mana base is designed to support the aggressive strategy with a mix of fetch lands and dual lands for consistency.

This deck revolves around using Basking Broodscale and Glaring Fleshraker to generate tokens and buff them with Blade of the Bloodchief. It also utilizes Emrakul, the Promised End and Writhing Chrysalis for additional threats. The deck includes cards like Kozilek's Command and Malevolent Rumble for removal, while cards like Vexing Bauble and Ancient Stirrings help with card selection. The deck aims to create a strong board presence while disrupting the opponent's game plan, ultimately overwhelming them with powerful creatures and combo interactions.

The deck aims to quickly reanimate powerful creatures like Griselbrand using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Ephemerate. The combo utilizes Atraxa, Grand Unifier to reduce the cost of spells, while cards like Psychic Frog and Quantum Riddler provide card advantage. The deck also includes disruption like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation to control the game and protect the combo. The mana base is carefully constructed to support the deck's color requirements and maximize consistency. By combining instant reanimation with efficient creatures and disruption, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents in Modern 1 vs 1 matches.

This deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones, and then bringing them back to the battlefield through reanimation spells like Persist and Unearth. The combo aspect comes from being able to cheat out these expensive creatures early in the game, overwhelming your opponent with powerful threats. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Thoughtseize and Spell Pierce to protect the combo. Overall, the strategy is to create a strong board presence and close out the game quickly before the opponent can recover.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures through card draw and mill effects, then using reanimation spells like Unearth and Persist to bring them back onto the battlefield for a strong and aggressive play. It also includes disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Spell Pierce to protect the combo and control the opponent's actions. The key strategy is to efficiently and effectively assemble the combo pieces while disrupting the opponent's game plan, ultimately overwhelming them with powerful creatures.
